problem_title: Adverse media results stay siloed from reviews
problem_description: Exchanges struggle to link adverse media alerts directly to onboarding and ongoing compliance decisions. This disconnect creates gaps in risk visibility, manual triage delays, and inconsistent audit evidence across AML operations.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY aggregates adverse media alerts with onboarding and review data, linking risk signals, case details, decisions and evidence into one streamlined workflow for exchange compliance teams.
workflow_title: From adverse media alerts to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY channels adverse media screening results through risk rules, alert triage, case creation, reviewer decisions and evidence preservation for compliance workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for exchange compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides exchange compliance teams with a connected layer that connects adverse media results to onboarding, case management, risk rules and reporting within a structured AML compliance workflow.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle adverse media alerts?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates adverse media alerts, applies internal risk policies, routes cases for review, records decision logs and links evidence to compliance cases for streamlined escalation and audit readiness.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit evidence by capturing decision logs, case histories and provider outputs in an organized, reviewable format that supports internal audits and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing screening tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY connects existing sanctions and adverse media screening providers, structuring their outputs into one compliance workflow without requiring replacement or direct integration changes.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace adverse media providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace adverse media providers but sits above them to connect their results, applying consistent policies and managing cases within a controlled compliance operating layer.
final_title: Connect adverse media results to compliance decisions
final_description: Review how SFTY links exchange adverse media screening with onboarding, case workflows and audit-ready evidence.
